elements.sent-2411-578: These MFPC scores are standardized using the sample mean and sample standard deviation of the MFPC scores from the training set.
elements.sent-2411-579: These scores are then used to predict the RUL at time $t^*$ using Eq (\ref{eq22 ). \begin{equation}\label{eq22} \begin{split} {RUL}_{test}=\exp \bigg(\Big[c_0^{k^*}+\sum_{k=1}^{H_{k^*}} \zeta_{test, h}^{k^*} c_k^{k^*}\Big]\times \Big[(1/(N-1))\sum_{i=1}^N\big(\ln{TTF}_i-(1/N)\sum_{i=1}^N \ln{TTF}_i\big)^2\Big] +(1/N)\sum_{i=1}^N\ln{TTF_i} \bigg) -t^* \end{split}\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-580: We present a simulation study that mimics the operational and diagnostic challenges of a deep-space habitat.
elements.sent-2411-581: In such systems, critical subsystems like life support, power generation, etc., are monitored by numerous sensors, and degradation can occur through multiple unknown failure modes.
elements.sent-2411-582: Due to the scarcity of in-mission failure data and the infeasibility of performing full-scale failure testing in space, simulation-based validation is pursued in this case study.
elements.sent-2411-583: This study enables us to systematically evaluate our methodology’s ability to: (1) identify latent failure modes in unlabeled multivariate sensor data, (2) select informative sensors specific to each mode, and (3) accurately predict remaining useful life (RUL).
elements.sent-2411-584: To emulate the complex telemetry environment of deep-space habitats, we use a modified version of the simulation model from \cite{Fang2017 to generate degradation signals for engineered systems monitored by 20 sensors.}
elements.sent-2411-585: Each system is assumed to experience one of two possible failure modes, FM 1 or FM 2, with only a subset of sensors being informative for degradation under each mode.
elements.sent-2411-586: This setup reflects the sensor redundancy and noise similar to onboard telemetry in a deep space habitat.
elements.sent-2411-587: We generate data for 200 systems per failure mode, using 160 systems for training and 40 for testing.
elements.sent-2411-588: To evaluate the robustness of our methodology, we simulate varying signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs) to mimic differences in sensor quality and environmental interference expected in long-duration missions.
elements.sent-2411-589: The resulting dataset includes multivariate sensor signals and the true time-to-failure (TTF), used for assessing prediction accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-590: The signals of the underlying degradation process for system $i$ exhibiting failure mode $k$ are generated according to Eq (\ref{eqSimulate1 ).}
elements.sent-2411-591: We set the failure thresholds for FM 1 and FM 2 as $D_1=2$ and $D_2=1.5$, respectively. \begin{equation}\label{eqSimulate1} s_i^k(t)=-\frac{\theta_i^k}{\ln t}\end{equation} We consider a scenario where only a subset of sensors is correlated with the underlying degradation process associated with a given failure mode.
elements.sent-2411-592: The $p^{th}$ sensor signal for the $i^{th}$ system exhibiting failure mode $k$ is generated using Eq (\ref{eqSimulate2 ). \begin{equation}\label{eqSimulate2} s_{i, p}^k(t)=-\frac{\theta_{i, p}^k}{\ln t}+\epsilon_{i, p}^k(t)\end{equation} In this Eq, $\epsilon_{i, p ^k(t) \sim N\left(0,\left(\sigma_k^p\right)^2\right)$ is a white noise process parameterized by $\sigma_k^p=\mu_k / S N R_p^k$, where $S N R_p^k$ is the signal-to-noise for sensor $p$ and failure mode $k$.}}
elements.sent-2411-593: A relative error is then computed using Eq (\ref{RUL ). \begin{equation}\label{RUL} \text { Relative Error }=\frac{\mid \text {Estimated Life} - \text {Actual Life} \mid}{\text {Actual Life}} \times 100 \%\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-594: To further validate our methodology in a realistic operational context, we evaluate its performance on a benchmark dataset that closely mirrors subsystem degradation in deep-space habitats.
elements.sent-2411-595: Specifically, we apply our approach to the NASA's Commercial Modular Aero-Propulsion System Simulation (C-MAPSS) dataset \cite{Saxena2008 .}
elements.sent-2411-596: C-MAPSS simulates degradation in the components of turbofan engines that share telemetry and fault characteristics with propulsion and environmental control systems of deep space habitats.
elements.sent-2411-597: C-MAPSS involves multivariate sensor monitoring from healthy operation to system failure, with no explicit failure mode labels and only partial degradation histories for test systems.

(2023), whose methodology assumes knowledge of a fraction of the training set and does perform sensor selection.}}

elements.sent-2411-599: In this paper, we presented a prognostics methodology tailored to the unique challenges of deep-space habitats.
elements.sent-2411-600: Complex engineering systems such as deep space habitats, are monitored by a large number of sensors and are susceptible to multiple failure modes that are often unlabeled.
elements.sent-2411-601: Our approach addresses the need for autonomous and accurate remaining useful life prediction in environments where expert diagnosis and ground-based maintenance are not feasible.
elements.sent-2411-602: In the offline phase, we extract failure-mode-specific features using component-aligned functional principal component analysis.
elements.sent-2411-603: These features are used by a novel expectation maximization algorithm to train a regression model that simultaneously clusters systems by failure mode and selects the most informative sensors for each mode.
elements.sent-2411-604: In the online phase, we use multivariate functional principal component analysis to reduce the dimensionality of real-time sensor data, diagnose the active failure mode, and predict remaining useful life using a time-varying weighted functional regression model.
elements.sent-2411-605: We validated our methodology using two case studies.
elements.sent-2411-606: The first is a simulated dataset that reflects key properties of deep-space habitats, including mode-specific degradation behavior, redundant sensors, and variable signal-to-noise ratios.
elements.sent-2411-607: Our method achieved strong performance in clustering failure modes, identifying informative sensors, and predicting remaining useful life with low relative error.
elements.sent-2411-608: The second case study applied our method to the C-MAPSS dataset, which we used to test real-world feasibility under partial degradation observation and unlabeled failure conditions.
elements.sent-2411-609: Our model outperformed baseline approaches in early-life prediction and produced interpretable results by selecting a minimal and relevant set of sensors.
elements.sent-2411-610: This work is based on the assumption of a linear relationship between the log of time-to-failure (TTF) and the sensor signals.
elements.sent-2411-611: A possibility for improvement would be to relax this assumption.
elements.sent-2411-612: Future work will focus on extending the methodology to nonlinear models to improve prediction accuracy and capture complex degradation trends in autonomous mission scenarios.
